title: IAM Firewall Policy Drift Incident-Response Runbook
task_scope: security operations / IAM firewall drift containment
operational_purpose: reconcile firewall-policy drift evidence, approved attenuation windows, and stateful environment risk into a deterministic containment queue

workflow_repair.token_check_scope: executable code only (exclude comments and docstrings)

canonicalization.end_ms_normalization: coerce end_ms to int after trim; invalid -> 0
canonicalization.muted_normalization: booleans unchanged; strings true/1/yes => true, all other strings => false; non-string/non-bool use Python bool(value)
canonicalization.dedupe_by_alert_id: keep highest end_ms; tie-break by severity rank p1>p2>p3>p4, then longer normalized signature, then lexicographically larger normalized env; if still tied keep first seen row in input order

freeze_overlap.freeze_canonicalization: normalize env/start/end, drop end<=start, compact overlap/touch intervals per env

reopen_windows.reopen_canonicalization: normalize env/scope/start/end, keep rows whose severity_scope is in scope_values, drop end<=start, compact overlap/touch intervals per (env,severity_scope)
reopen_windows.matching_scopes: {all,max_severity} for each window; if max_severity is p2 and (env,p2) has no compacted intervals, borrow (env,p1) as the severity scope fallback
reopen_windows.reopen_overlap_union: collect overlap segments from matching scopes then compact/union those segments to compute reopen_overlap_ms and reopen_segment_count
reopen_windows.stability_pressure_score: probe window [end_ms-180, end_ms+1) using reopen all + severity scopes; score=(all_probe_ms//30)+(severity_probe_ms//20)+max(alert_count-1,0)

rotation_windows.rotation_canonicalization: normalize env/scope/start/end, keep rows whose severity_scope is in scope_values, drop end<=start, compact overlap/touch intervals per (env,severity_scope)
rotation_windows.matching_scopes: {all,max_severity} for each window; if max_severity is p2 and (env,p2) has no compacted intervals, borrow (env,p1) as the severity scope fallback
rotation_windows.rotation_overlap_union: collect overlap segments from matching scopes then compact/union those segments to compute rotation_overlap_ms and rotation_segment_count

defer_windows.defer_canonicalization: normalize env/scope/start/end, keep rows whose severity_scope is in scope_values, drop end<=start, compact overlap/touch intervals per (env,severity_scope)
defer_windows.matching_scopes: {all,max_severity} for each window; if max_severity is p2 and (env,p2) has no compacted intervals, borrow (env,p1) as the severity scope fallback
defer_windows.defer_overlap_union: collect overlap segments from matching scopes then compact/union those segments to compute defer_overlap_ms and defer_segment_count

probe_scoring_contract.interval_semantics: all source intervals and probes are half-open; overlap is max(0,min(probe_end,interval_end)-max(probe_start,interval_start))
probe_scoring_contract.scope_accounting: all_probe_ms uses only the already-compacted (env,all) intervals; severity_probe_ms uses already-compacted (env,max_severity) intervals, except p2 windows fall back to (env,p1) when (env,p2) is empty
probe_scoring_contract.cross_scope_rule: do not union or deduplicate all-scope probe overlap against severity-scope probe overlap; an instant covered by both scopes contributes independently to both integer-division terms
probe_scoring_contract.within_scope_rule: source intervals are compacted within each (env,severity_scope) before probing, so overlap is not duplicated within one scope
probe_scoring_contract.probe_end_rule: the +1 endpoint is literal: an anchor E with lookback L probes [E-L,E+1), whose duration is L+1 milliseconds before clipping

risk_ledger.evaluation_scope: state is independent per normalized env
risk_ledger.evaluation_order: process each env's merged windows in start_ms ascending order after all four attenuation layers are complete
risk_ledger.state_update: finalize carry_out_ms for one window before evaluating the next window in the same env

worked_example_no_attenuation.first_window: lab [100,400): actionable=300, idle_gap=0, carry_in=0, carry_out=min(0+300,2000)=300, ledger_adjusted=300

iam_trust_exposure.canonicalization: normalize source_env and target_env with canonicalization.env_normalization; coerce weight with int(str(value).strip()) and invalid to 0; discard self edges and weights outside 1..9; collapse duplicate directed (source_env,target_env) rows by maximum weight
iam_trust_exposure.graph_direction: edges are directed from source_env to target_env
iam_trust_exposure.path_domain: for each drift window, begin at its normalized env and enumerate simple directed paths of one, two, or three edges; a simple path never repeats a node, so cycles are bounded and the origin cannot reappear
iam_trust_exposure.path_score: sum canonical edge weights along the path
iam_trust_exposure.strongest_per_target: for each reachable target retain the path with greatest path_score; on equal score retain the lexicographically smallest full node sequence
iam_trust_exposure.trust_reachable_envs: all retained target names sorted ascending
iam_trust_exposure.trust_exposure_score: sum retained strongest path scores across trust_reachable_envs; use 0 when no target is reachable
iam_trust_exposure.trust_strongest_path: among retained target paths choose greatest path_score, then lexicographically smallest full node sequence; use [origin_env] when no target is reachable
iam_trust_exposure.trust_path_digest: first 12 lowercase hex chars of sha256(origin|trust_exposure_score|semicolon_joined_target_rows), where each target row in trust_reachable_envs order is target:score:path_nodes_joined_by_>
iam_trust_exposure.priority_integration: critical when trust_exposure_score>=24; otherwise high when trust_exposure_score>=12, in addition to existing rules

priority_rules.critical: max_severity == p1 and ledger_adjusted_actionable_ms >= 235, or ledger_adjusted_actionable_ms >= 500, or stability_index >= 20, or trust_exposure_score >= 24
priority_rules.high: ledger_adjusted_actionable_ms >= 265, or alert_count >= 3 with max_severity in {p1,p2}, or rotation_segment_count == 0 with risk_adjusted_duration_ms >= 340, or defer_pressure_score > 0 with dispatchable_duration_ms >= 320, or reopen_segment_count == 0 with duration_ms >= 420, or trust_exposure_score >= 12

response_queue.json_format: compact JSON lines via json.dumps(..., separators=(',', ':'))

summary_json.total_unmuted_duration_ms_contract: sum of merged window duration_ms values from drift_windows.json (not raw alert durations)
summary_json.total_ledger_adjusted_actionable_ms_cont: sum ledger_adjusted_actionable_ms over all drift windows, including windows not admitted to the response queue

maximum_aggregation_domains.queue_only: max_stability_pressure_score, max_volatility_index, max_defer_pressure_score, max_ledger_pressure_score, max_trust_exposure_score, and max_stability_index are maxima over final admitted response_queue rows only; use 0 when the queue is empty
maximum_aggregation_domains.all_drift_windows: max_carry_out_ms is the maximum over every drift window, including windows not admitted to the response queue; use 0 when there are no windows

checksum_serialization_contract.trailing_delimiter: none: do not append a newline or pipe after the final row
checksum_serialization_contract.muted_encoding: integer text 1 when normalized muted is true, otherwise integer text 0; never serialize JSON true/false
checksum_serialization_contract.canonical_alert_rows: one alert_id|env|start_ms|end_ms|severity|muted_int|signature row for each canonical deduplicated alert, sorted by (env ascending, start_ms ascending, alert_id ascending)
checksum_serialization_contract.freeze_rows: one env|start_ms|end_ms row for each compacted interval, sorted by env ascending and then interval (start_ms,end_ms) ascending
checksum_serialization_contract.scoped_rows: one env|severity_scope|start_ms|end_ms row for reopen/rotation/defer using only compacted rows whose severity_scope is in that source's scope_values allowlist; sort by (env,severity_scope) ascending and then interval (start_ms,end_ms) ascending
checksum_serialization_contract.trust_edge_rows: one source_env|target_env|weight row per canonical deduplicated directed edge, sorted by source_env then target_env ascending
checksum_serialization_contract.ledger_rows: one env|start_ms|idle_gap_ms|carry_in_ms|carry_out_ms|ledger_adjusted_actionable_ms row for every drift window, sorted by env ascending and start_ms ascending

summary_json.reopen_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of checksum_serialization_contract scoped_rows built from compacted reopen intervals
summary_json.rotation_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of checksum_serialization_contract scoped_rows built from compacted rotation intervals
summary_json.defer_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of checksum_serialization_contract scoped_rows built from compacted defer intervals
summary_json.queue_hash_checksum: sha256('|'.join(queue_hash values in final queue order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.window_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(window_digest values in final queue order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.trust_path_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(trust_path_digest values in final queue order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
